task_4_1_grouping_prompt: You are a software requirements engineering assistant. You are given cleaned Google Play app reviews for a mental health app. Group the reviews into exactly 5 meaningful thematic groups. Every group must be non-empty. Every group must contain at least 10 review IDs. The 5 themes should cover: (1) mood tracking and self-reflection, (2) mental health support and stress management, (3) informational content and insights, (4) pricing and paid feature limitations, and (5) privacy, trust, and data concerns. Do not place pricing, subscription, refund, paywall, premium, or cost complaints inside the informational content group. Do not place lost data, app crashes, sync issues, or access problems inside the privacy group unless the review explicitly discusses privacy, trust, sharing, or data handling. Use only the reviews provided. Do not invent review IDs. Return valid JSON only in this exact format: {"groups": [{"group_id": "G1", "theme": "...", "review_ids": ["minddoc_1", "minddoc_2"], "example_reviews": ["...", "..."]}]}
task_4_2_persona_prompt: You are a software requirements engineering assistant. Based on the grouped review themes, generate exactly 5 personas, one per group. Each persona must reference the review group it was derived from and follow the personas_manual.json style. Every persona must include at least 2 evidence review IDs taken from that group's review_ids. Do not invent unsupported constraints, goals, or context. Keep each persona grounded in the grouped review evidence. Return valid JSON only in this exact format: {"personas": [{"id": "PA1", "name": "...", "description": "...", "derived_from_group": "G1", "goals": ["..."], "pain_points": ["..."], "context": ["..."], "constraints": ["..."], "evidence_reviews": ["minddoc_1", "minddoc_2"]}]}
